clips.desc: Record stream moments. The global shortcut Ctrl+Shift+M works even when the app is minimized.

mobile.appDesc: The mobile app includes live chat, TTS controls (pause, skip, toggles) and clip markers.

obs.hint: OBS > Tools > WebSocket Server → enable on port 4455.

clipHint: The global shortcut works even when the app is minimized.

soundpad.desc: Upload sounds and play them with a click or keyboard shortcut (works in fullscreen).
soundpad.empty: Upload your first sound to get started

bot.title: Music Bot
bot.desc: Chat can request songs with !p name or !p YouTube link. Your playlist plays in the background when there are no requests.
bot.playlistHint: One song per line: name or YouTube URL. Plays when chat has no requests.

overlay2.giftsSubtitle: Appears in OBS for a few seconds when someone sends a gift
overlay2.socialSubtitle: Appears in OBS for a few seconds when someone follows or shares
overlay2.creditosSubtitle: Auto-scroll with donors, followers and shares
overlay2.socialListSubtitle: Cumulative panel visible throughout the stream in OBS

clips2.confirmClearAll: Delete all clip markers? This action cannot be undone.
clips2.noMarkers: No markers yet. Start the stream and press Ctrl+Shift+M to mark.

conn.noSavedChannels: No saved channels. Add TikTok/Twitch/YouTube and use Connect all.

adv.subtitle: Server Settings
adv.desc: Control rate limiting, message filters and monitor system status in real time. Changes apply immediately to the backend.
adv.rlHowP1: During an active live, dozens of messages per second can arrive. Without limits, the TTS queue would grow uncontrollably and the bot would accumulate minutes of delay.
adv.rlHowP2: Rate limiting acts as a server-side filter: it decides how many TTS requests are allowed in a time window. If the limit is exceeded, additional requests are rejected with error 429 and the message does not enter the voice queue.
adv.rlHowP3: It is DISABLED by default. Enable it only if you see the bot saturating or if Google TTS starts giving errors due to too many requests.
adv.rlHowP4: Practical example: with 10 requests / 5 seconds, if 20 messages arrive in 5 seconds, only the first 10 will be read. The rest are silently discarded.
adv.rlToggleHint: Disabled by default. Only enable it if the live has a lot of spam or TTS gets saturated.
adv.filtersExplainerP1: The server already filters automatically: repetitive messages (aaaaaa), URLs (replaced by "link"), mentions (@user removed), emojis (converted to space) and messages over 300 characters.
adv.filtersExplainerP2: Here you can add additional words to block messages containing them.
adv.blockedDesc: Messages containing these words will be completely ignored by TTS. The filter is case-insensitive.
adv.blockedPlaceholder: Type a word and press Add...
adv.mdDesc: You can edit the blocked-words.md file directly here or upload a new one. Format: one word per line starting with - .
adv.logsClearConfirm: Clear all logs? This cannot be undone.
